role_title: Member of Technical Staff AI Policy and Strategic Initiatives
secondary_archetype: Technical Staff / AI Policy & Strategic Initiatives hybrid (engineering + policy + legal + ops)

seniority.level: Early-career to Experienced IC (new grads to experienced engineers)
level_signals: Exceptionally talented early-career engineers (new grads welcome)
Experienced engineers at top tech companies who wish to develop policy, business, and operations chops
Lawyers or economists (new JD or PhD grads welcome) who've kept their engineering skills sharp
Minimum 2-year commitment mentioned for those planning future graduate school

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Evidence of exceptional ability and 99th-percentile work ethic.
Strong technical skills at or above Perplexity's hiring bar for software engineers.
Fluency with frontier intelligence tools (such as Perplexity Computer).
High intrinsically-enforced standards for the craft and quality of your work.
Strong communication and writing skills, ideally demonstrated via samples of past written work product.
Curiosity and excitement about new and unfamiliar fields; eagerness to redefine your job every 3-6 months.
Ability to quickly dive deep in any given field and develop mastery rivaling that of domain experts.
Unimpeachable judgment, integrity, and discretion to be entrusted with the company's most important matters.
A service-oriented attitude that continually questions how company systems and processes might better serve stakeholders.
we ask for a minimum 2-year commitment (i.e., you would apply for the entering class of 2028 or later).

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: well-read in law, tech policy, or economics, and (2) wish to grow in both technical and non-technical disciplines.
Experienced engineers at top tech companies who wish to develop policy, business, and operations chops.
Lawyers or economists (new JD or PhD grads welcome) who've kept their engineering skills sharp and are excited to reimagine their fields in the age of AI.

scope.surface: AI policy, law, operations, corporate affairs, intellectual property, privacy/compliance, litigation support, regulatory filings, recruiting/HR systems
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality and impact of regulatory and policy contributions
Scale and compliance of privacy systems
Support outcomes for high-tech litigation matters
cross_functional_partners: Capitol Hill and executive branch officials
core_responsibilities: Harness AI agents to research high-stakes policy and regulatory issues pertaining to Perplexity
Build automated platforms for harvesting, prioritizing, and patenting Perplexity's inventions
Work hands-on with our Legal team and outside counsel in support of high-tech litigation matters in U.S. federal court
Engineer privacy and compliance systems that efficiently scale with our growing portfolio of frontier AI products
Contribute to regulatory filings and responses that require impeccable command of AI technology
Embed with our People team to implement first-party software solutions for recruiting, labor compliance, and performance management
Participate in meetings with Capitol Hill, executive branch officials, international partners, and civil society as a subject-matter expert

green_flags: Explicitly welcomes new grads and early-career candidates
Welcomes diverse profiles including lawyers, economists, and engineers
Reports to senior leadership (VP Global Affairs & Deputy CTO) — high visibility role
Described as uniquely interdisciplinary with broad growth opportunities
Mentions explicit mentorship: 'a bench of mentors who will push you to learn and grow day after day'
Accommodates candidates planning future law school or PhD (2-year minimum commitment)
red_flags: No location or remote policy mentioned anywhere in the JD
Role is extremely broad and ill-defined — 'no two days will be the same' may indicate unclear scope or shifting priorities
Archetype is genuinely novel and hard to classify — candidates may struggle to evaluate fit
2-year minimum commitment requirement may limit flexibility for candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from candidate profile descriptions; no explicit level stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product descriptions: Comet, Computer)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation (no compensation data anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: The role is described as 'Member of Technical Staff' but encompasses policy, legal, operations, and corporate affairs — not a conventional engineering or PM role; archetype set to 'Other' accordingly.
The candidate profiles described are highly varied (new grad engineers, experienced engineers, JDs, PhDs, economists) making seniority level ambiguous.
The 'ideally demonstrated via samples of past written work product' qualifier on communication/writing skills is borderline hard/soft; treated as hard given overall framing.
It is unclear whether the 2-year commitment requirement applies only to candidates planning graduate school or to all candidates; raw text suggests it applies to the former subset only.
